Track When ANY Video is Uploaded to Youtube W/ This Extension | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 10
videos.description:
I exploited YouTube's own internal search API to build a Chrome extension that monitors all of YouTube in the background, and it notifies you the moment a new video matching your keywords goes live. No API keys.

Get the extension → https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/youtube-keyword-alert-get/pniolepdakiocafjiibgiabkcdhgkfep

How it works
You set up a keyword filter. The extension scans YouTube every hour using YouTube's own internal API, completely in the background. The moment a new video goes live matching your keywords, you get a Chrome notification instantly.

For example:

Let's say you want to track GTA 6 leaks. But you're tired of fake footage from tiny channels. Add the keywords 'gta 6', 'leak', and 'gameplay', set a minimum of 10,000 subscribers, exclude the channels you hate - and you'll know the second something real drops. Before YouTube's algorithm even thinks about showing it to you.

TIMESTAMPS:
00:00 — What this extension does
01:03 — Why I built it
01:19 — Building the hello world script
01:39 — Planning the extension with Claude
01:52 — First working version
02:00 — The CORS battle
02:14 — UI and design with ChatGPT + Claude
02:37 — Export/import, bug fixes and final touches
02:50 — Generating a permanent extension ID
03:00 — Logo, description and publishing

--

I share all my extension's live stats here: https://codedcitadel.com/live-stats

videos.title: Instagram Can BAN This Extension... | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 9
videos.description:
I found an Instagram comments exporter on the Chrome Web Store that was almost good, but broke on long extraction sessions, mangled the dates, and skipped GIFs entirely. So I rebuilt it from scratch with AI doing most of the heavy lifting.

In this episode: reverse-engineering Instagram's private API from the network tab, pulling the richer GraphQL endpoint instead of the basic one, cloning Instagram's own UI pixel-for-pixel so the extension feels native, and shipping export to CSV,  XLSX, JSON, and TXT, GIFs included.

Built in about 8 hours and 35 Cursor prompts (not counting the Claude conversations).

Get the extension: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/instagram-comments-exporter
Full technical breakdown: [link]
Live install stats for all my extensions: https://codedcitadel.com/live-stats

This is episode 9 of Coding Until I Make $100K, where I build and ship stuff on camera until the series hits six figures in revenue.

Stack: Cursor, Claude, Supabase, Chrome Extension APIs (Manifest V3)

videos.title: Bulk Export Gmail to PDF (With Attachments) | Senior Dev Coding to $100k USD | Ep. 8
videos.description:
Gmail has no native way to export emails as PDF. The Chrome extensions I was using for years stopped working, the ones with good reviews require a login I don't want to give, and most of them inject permanent buttons into the Gmail UI that I find disruptive. So I built my own.

In this episode I walk you through how I built a Chrome extension to bulk export Gmail emails as PDF, HTML, TXT and JSON - including images and attachments downloaded as a .zip - in ~15 hours and with minimal permissions.

No login required. No third-party account. No unnecessary permissions.

---

🔖 Blog (thorough technical details): https://codedcitadel.com/blog/ep-8-gmail-to-pdf
📧 Install Gmail to PDF:https://codedcitadel.com/apps/gmail-to-pdf

---

⏱ TIMESTAMPS

0:00 - What this extension does
0:49 - Why I stopped trusting Gmail PDF extensions
1:24 - Starting in the browser console (not Cursor)
1:44 - Finding email data in Gmail's network tab
2:03 - Reverse-engineering Gmail's fetch API with Claude
2:13 - HTML export working, attachments broken
2:27 - Base64 explained + fixing image quality
2:45 - Moving to Cursor and writing the prompt
2:55 - Chrome extension permissions (what to avoid)
3:09 - Injecting a native-looking button into Gmail's toolbar
3:36 - The ironic part: PDF was the hardest export format
3:46 - html2pdf working on small emails, failing on large ones
4:03 - The fix: waiting for the document to fully load
4:07 - Logo design with ChatGPT
4:17 - Final result + wrap up

---

🔍 WHAT THIS VIDEO COVERS

How to export Gmail emails to PDF | Bulk Gmail PDF export Chrome extension | Save Gmail emails as PDF without login | Gmail to PDF with attachments | Export Gmail to HTML, TXT and JSON | Reverse-engineering Gmail's internal API | How to use html2pdf in a Chrome extension | Chrome extension minimal permissions | How to inject buttons into Gmail UI | base64 image inlining for PDF export

---

📌 ABOUT THIS SERIES

Senior Dev Coding Until I Make $100k is a build-in-public series where I document every Chrome extension I ship - the idea, the code, the bugs and the publishing process - until I hit $100k in revenue. No fluff, just the real process.

New episodes every week.

videos.title: Save Your Favorite AI Replies in One Place (100% FREE) | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 7
videos.description:
AI gives great answers sometimes. The problem is keeping track of them across Claude, ChatGPT, and Grok. So I built a Chrome extension to bookmark any reply, from any AI, and manage everything in one place.

This was the most complex extension on this channel so far - more than double the hours of the previous record. Most of that time went into the authentication system. Supabase makes things easier, but Google login inside a Chrome extension has a few very specific gotchas that will slow you down no matter how many times you've done it.

All the technical details and bugs are covered in the blog post linked below. The video focuses on the building process.

IN THIS EPISODE:
Finding a unique CSS selector to identify individual AI replies and inject a bookmark button
Getting the working script for Claude, then doing the same for ChatGPT and Grok
Designing the UI with Claude and ChatGPT, then sending the design as an image to Claude to code the HTML/CSS/JS
Building the full Chrome extension and wiring it up with Supabase
Implementing the "scroll back to bookmarked reply" function
Setting up Google login and generating a permanent Chrome extension ID
Logo, final UX polish, and submitting to the Chrome Web Store

TECH STACK:
Vite + Manifest V3 + Vanilla JS + Supabase + Google OAuth

LINKS:
AI Bookmark on Chrome Web Store: [link — coming soon, pending review]
Full technical blog post: [link]
Coded Citadel: https://codedcitadel.com
GitHub: [link]

TIMESTAMPS:
0:00 - Intro
1:08 - Ideating the concept and finding the CSS selector
2:00 - Same script for ChatGPT and Grok
2:18 - Designing the UI with Claude and ChatGPT
3:01 - Building the Chrome extension and wiring up Supabase
3:22 - Grok and ChatGPT versions + bookmark manager
3:35 - Scroll back to bookmarked reply
3:42 - Google login and permanent extension ID
4:28 - UX polish, logo, and submitting to the Chrome Web Store

videos.title: I "Hacked" Claude to Track My Usage Limit | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 6
videos.description:
Claude keeps cutting you off mid-thought. So I built a Chrome extension that shows 
you exactly how much of your limit is left - and reverse engineered Claude's website 
to make it work.

In this video I break down how I found Claude's internal usage API, how the extension 
intercepts the response stream to read your messages remaining in real time, and how 
I built and shipped the whole thing.

Install Claude Limit Monitor → https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/claude-limit-monitor-trac/mljfhcfnjbfibedpiaheeihpbjajfcal

videos.title: I Turned Google Drive Into My Browser's Download Folder | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep.5
videos.description:
I found a Chrome extension idea on Reddit, built it in 8 hours, and submitted it to the Chrome Web Store, all in one session. In this video I walk you through the full process: finding the idea, architecting the extension, handling Google OAuth in the background script, building the right-click save functionality, and designing the UI using AI tools.

This is Episode 5 of Senior Dev Coding to $100k - a series where I build and ship Chrome extensions from scratch and document everything publicly.

What I cover:
- How I found the idea browsing Reddit
- Setting up Google OAuth correctly in a Chrome extension (always in background.js, never the popup)
- Getting a permanent Chrome extension ID for development
- Using Cursor to build the upload functionality
- Designing the UI with ShadeGPT and coding it with Claude
- Creating the logo with AI
- Submitting to the Chrome Web Store

Tech used: Chrome Extensions, Google Drive API, Google OAuth, Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, JavaScript

Download: still waiting for Chrome webstore's approval

videos.title: Export Instagram Conversations in One Click | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 4
videos.description:
No Instagram API key needed, 100% free. 

I built a Chrome extension that exports any Instagram conversation to HTML or TXT in one click - by reverse engineering how Instagram loads messages directly in the browser.

In this episode I walk through the full process: finding a crowded niche and spotting a gap anyway, reverse engineering Instagram's internal API using the network tab, using Claude to write the pagination fetch loop, and my exact workflow for injecting UI that blends natively into Instagram's design.

This is episode 4 of my build-in-public series shipping Chrome extensions on the road to $100k. No sales yet - but that's not the point right now.

Extension live on the Chrome Web Store (link below)
Subscribe to follow the journey

Download: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/instagram-dm-exporter/hgojieiehkjgjhdnbglfhbcojeeggigi
Blog with more details: https://medium.com/@codedcitadel/how-i-reverse-engineered-instagram-to-export-dms-no-api-key-needed-e8ed7a05b657

videos.title: How to Deep Search In Claude | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 3
videos.description:
Claude's search only lets you search conversation titles - not the actual content inside them. So I built a free Chrome extension to fix it. Total time: 3 hours, including the extension, logo, screenshots, and a landing page.

In this episode I walk through the full process: how I accidentally stumbled onto the idea while doing product research, how I validated it in minutes, how I reverse engineered Claude's internal API to fetch full conversation content, and my exact step-by-step workflow for shipping a Chrome extension as fast as possible.

If you use Claude daily and have ever lost a conversation because you couldn't search what was actually inside it - this extension is for you, and it's completely free.

Extension live on the Chrome Web Store: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/claude-deep-search
Subscribe to follow the $100k journey!

videos.title: How I Unlocked YouTube's "Hidden" Filters | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 2
videos.description:
The only way through is forward!

In Episode 2 of my Senior Dev Coding to $100k series, I'm building my second Chrome extension - a YouTube video filter that lets you sort search results by view count, video length, and more. But before writing a single line of code, I walk through my data-driven product research process: why I scraped Etsy sellers on Reddit, what I found (and didn't find), and how a frustrating YouTube search session gave me the idea.

I break down the full build process - reverse engineering YouTube's internal JSON API using the browser network tab, generating a native-looking YouTube UI with ChatGPT and YouTube's own CSS variables, and using Claude to build clean HTML/CSS. I also document the debugging rabbit hole that cost me hours: YouTube's pagination token system and why I was silently fetching invisible videos the whole time.

This is a no-cuts, honest look at how a solo developer ships a working Chrome extension MVP from idea to live product - bugs, dead ends, and all.

If you're interested in Chrome extension development, building in public, or making money with side projects in 2025, this series is for you.

Subscribe for weekly build logs.

Download: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/youtube-filter-pro-filter/dbkkcbfafkckhmefkpgnelikibobcabb
Blog with more details: https://medium.com/@codedcitadel/how-to-filter-youtube-videos-by-views-i-reverse-engineered-youtubes-api-to-build-this-977d9200eeff
--

TIMESTAMPS

0:49 - Why I use data instead of creativity alone for product ideas

videos.title: This is How I Plan to Make $100k by Coding | Senior Dev Coding to $100k | Ep. 1
videos.description:
This is bound to be a long journey.

Episode 1 of my Senior Dev Coding to $100k - and we're starting at $0. I'm a software engineer with 10+ years of experience, and I'm documenting every step of building a Chrome extension portfolio from scratch using AI tools like Claude and ChatGPT.

In this first episode I lay out the full strategy: why I'm starting with Chrome extensions (low competition, no hosting costs, built-in discovery via the Chrome Web Store), why I'm launching everything free first to build traction, and how I'm using AI as my co-developer throughout the entire process.

Then I build the first extension live - a YouTube Comments Scraper that lets you bulk export comments from individual videos or entire playlists, and send them straight to Claude or ChatGPT for analysis. This tool is the backbone of my product research system for finding real user pain points before building anything.

I also walk through my full workflow: designing UIs with ChatGPT, coding HTML/CSS with Claude using BEM methodology, wiring up Supabase Edge Functions to keep API keys secure, building a smart review/feedback modal system, and packaging the extension for the Chrome Web Store.
No fluff. No funnel. Just a real build-in-public series.

Subscribe to follow the journey weekly!

App made in this episode: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/yt-comments-exporter
All apps: https://codedcitadel.com/apps/

---
